rationale: The citing paper does more than list the 2008 article as background; it summarizes its core finding that similarity and overlap with Wal-Mart hurt incumbent performance and reuses that insight in its moderator development. In the moderators section, Gielens et al. is explicitly invoked to motivate why assortment overlap should matter, and the citing paper empirically tests related overlap effects on supplier profits and shipments. The citation also reappears in interpretation and managerial implications to contextualize results about overlap-driven vulnerability.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Gielens et al.'s incumbent-focused Wal-Mart entry evidence, especially the detrimental role of overlap with Wal-Mart. It extends that line by examining whether similar overlap patterns explain heterogeneous effects on supplier performance.
